metadata.title: Guideline on Similar Biological Medicinal Products Containing Biotechnology-Derived Proteins as Active Substance: Non-clinical and Clinical Issues
metadata.notes: No prior extraction found in output-v2, v3, or v4. This is Rev1 (2014) replacing the original 2005 version. Full extraction performed.

rules.rule_text: The Marketing Authorisation application dossier of a biosimilar medicinal product must provide a full quality dossier together with data demonstrating comparability with the reference medicinal product using appropriate physico-chemical and in vitro biological tests, non-clinical studies, and clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Marketing Authorisation (MA) application dossier of a biosimilar medicinal product shall provide a full quality dossier together with data demonstrating comparability with the reference medicinal product by using appropriate physico-chemical and in vitro biological tests, non-clinical studies and clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: If the biosimilar comparability exercise indicates relevant differences making it unlikely that biosimilarity will be established, a stand-alone development to support a full Marketing Authorisation Application must be considered instead.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the biosimilar comparability exercise indicates that there are relevant differences between the biosimilar and the reference medicinal product, making it unlikely that biosimilarity will eventually be established, a stand-alone development to support a full Marketing Authorisation Application, should be considered instead.

rules.rule_text: Relevant non-clinical studies must be performed before initiating clinical trials; in vitro pharmaco-toxicological studies must be conducted first, followed by determination of whether in vivo animal studies are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: To support biosimilarity, relevant non-clinical studies should be performed before initiating clinical trials. A stepwise approach is recommended for evaluation of the similarity of the biosimilar and the reference product. Analytical studies... and in vitro pharmaco-toxicological studies should be conducted first and a decision then made as to the extent of what, if any, in vivo work in animal studies will be required.

rules.rule_text: In vitro studies must include assays on: binding to target(s) involved in pharmaco-toxicological effects and/or pharmacokinetics, and signal transduction and functional activity/viability of cells relevant to the pharmaco-toxicological effects of the reference product.
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies should include relevant assays on: - Binding to target(s) (e.g. receptors, antigens, enzymes) known to be involved in the pharmaco-toxicological effects and/or pharmacokinetics of the reference product. - Signal transduction and functional activity/viability of cells known to be of relevance for the pharmaco-toxicological effects of the reference product.

rules.rule_text: In vitro studies must be sensitive, specific and sufficiently discriminatory to provide evidence that observed differences in quality attributes are clinically not relevant; they must compare concentration-activity/binding relationships at the pharmacological target(s) covering a range where potential differences are most sensitively detected.
rules.verbatim_quote: The studies should be sensitive, specific and sufficiently discriminatory to provide evidence that observed differences in quality attributes are clinically not relevant. The studies should compare the concentration–activity/binding relationship of the biosimilar and the reference medicinal product at the pharmacological target(s), covering a concentration range where potential differences are most sensitively detected.

rules.rule_text: Standard repeated dose toxicity studies in non-human primates are usually not recommended; studies regarding safety pharmacology, reproduction toxicology, and carcinogenicity are not required for non-clinical testing of biosimilars.
rules.verbatim_quote: The conduct of standard repeated dose toxicity studies in non-human primates is usually not recommended... Studies regarding safety pharmacology, reproduction toxicology, and carcinogenicity are not required for non-clinical testing of biosimilars.
rules.exceptions: If appropriately justified, a repeated dose toxicity study with refined design may be considered. Local tolerance may need evaluation if novel excipients are introduced.

rules.rule_text: Clinical data for the biosimilar comparability exercise must be generated with the biosimilar product derived from the commercial manufacturing process; any deviation must be justified and supported by adequate bridging data per ICH Q5E.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to generate the clinical data required for the biosimilar comparability exercise with the biosimilar product derived from the commercial manufacturing process and therefore representing the quality profile of the batches to become commercialised. Any deviation from this recommendation should be justified and supported by adequate additional bridging data (as described in guideline ICH Q5E).
rules.exceptions: Deviations must be justified and supported by bridging data

rules.rule_text: The clinical biosimilar comparability exercise must normally begin with pharmacokinetic (PK) and, if feasible, pharmacodynamic (PD) studies followed by clinical efficacy and safety trial(s), or confirmatory PK/PD studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical biosimilar comparability exercise is normally a stepwise procedure that should begin with pharmacokinetic (PK) and, if feasible, pharmacodynamic (PD) studies followed by clinical efficacy and safety trial(s) or, in certain cases, confirmatory PK / PD studies for demonstrating clinical biosimilar comparability.
rules.exceptions: In certain cases, confirmatory PK/PD studies may be sufficient if specific conditions are met

rules.rule_text: The biosimilar comparability limits for the main PK parameters must be defined and justified prior to conducting the study; observing 90% CIs within a justified acceptance range alone may not be sufficient — the location and width of the CI must also be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: The biosimilar comparability limits for the main PK parameters should be defined and justified prior to conducting the study... This means that observing 90% CIs of ratios of biosimilar to reference product within a pre-specified, justified acceptance range may not, by itself, be sufficient. The location and the width of the confidence interval should also be taken into account in the interpretation of similarity.
rules.exceptions: Correction for protein content may be acceptable on a case-by-case basis if pre-specified and adequately justified

rules.rule_text: In a single dose PK study, the primary parameters are AUC(0-inf) for intravenous administration and AUC(0-inf) and usually Cmax for subcutaneous administration; anti-drug antibodies must be measured in parallel with PK assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: In a single dose PK study, the primary parameters are the AUC(0-inf) for intravenous administration and AUC(0-inf) and usually Cmax for subcutaneous administration. Secondary parameters such as tmax, volume of distribution, and half-life, should also be estimated... In any PK study, anti-drug antibodies should be measured in parallel to PK assessment using appropriate sampling time points.
rules.exceptions: In multiple dose studies: primary parameters are truncated AUC(0-t) and AUC over dosage interval at steady state

rules.rule_text: Comparative PK/PD studies may be sufficient to demonstrate clinical comparability if: (1) the PD marker is an accepted surrogate related to patient outcome, or (2) PD markers with a clear dose-response or concentration-response relationship are available, enabling a single or multiple dose-exposure-response study to waive clinical efficacy study.
rules.verbatim_quote: In certain cases, comparative PK/PD studies may be sufficient to demonstrate clinical comparability of the biosimilar and the reference medicinal product, provided that the following conditions are met: The selected PD marker/biomarker is an accepted surrogate marker and can be related to patient outcome to the extent that demonstration of similar effect on the PD marker will ensure a similar effect on the clinical outcome.
rules.exceptions: In exceptional cases, confirmatory clinical trial may be waived if physicochemical, structural and in vitro biological analyses and human PK studies together with PD markers provide robust evidence

rules.rule_text: In the absence of surrogate markers for efficacy, comparable clinical efficacy must be demonstrated in adequately powered, randomised, parallel group comparative clinical trials, preferably double-blind, using efficacy endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the absence of surrogate markers for efficacy, it is usually necessary to demonstrate comparable clinical efficacy of the biosimilar and the reference medicinal product in adequately powered, randomised, parallel group comparative clinical trial(s), preferably double-blind, by using efficacy endpoints.
rules.exceptions: A non-inferiority design may be acceptable if justified on the basis of a strong scientific rationale

rules.rule_text: An equivalence design should generally be used for efficacy trials; non-inferiority may only be accepted where the possibility of a significant and clinically relevant increase in efficacy can be excluded on scientific and mechanistic grounds; use of non-inferiority must be discussed with regulatory authorities.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, an equivalence design should be used. The use of a non-inferiority design may be acceptable if justified on the basis of a strong scientific rationale... A non-inferiority trial may only be accepted where the possibility of significant and clinically relevant increase in efficacy can be excluded on scientific and mechanistic grounds. It is recommended to discuss the use of a non-inferiority design with regulatory authorities.

rules.rule_text: Comparability margins must be pre-specified and justified on both statistical and clinical grounds using data of the reference product; clinical data cannot be used to justify substantial differences in quality attributes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comparability margins should be pre-specified and justified on both statistical and clinical grounds by using the data of the reference product... Clinical data cannot be used to justify substantial differences in quality attributes.

rules.rule_text: Immunogenicity testing must be conducted within the biosimilar comparability exercise using the same assay format and sampling schedule for both the biosimilar and reference product; assays must be capable of detecting antibodies against both the biosimilar and reference molecules.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity testing of the biosimilar and the reference product should be conducted within the biosimilar comparability exercise by using the same assay format and sampling schedule which must meet all current standards. Analytical assays should be performed with both the reference and biosimilar molecule in parallel... The analytical assays should preferably be capable of detecting antibodies against both the biosimilar and the reference molecule but should at least be able to detect all antibodies developed against the biosimilar molecule.

rules.rule_text: Extrapolation of clinical data to other therapeutic indications of the reference product is possible but must be scientifically justified; additional data are required when the active substance interacts with several receptors with different impacts, has more than one active site, or the studied indication is not sensitive for differences in all relevant aspects of efficacy and safety.
rules.source_section: Section 6 - Extrapolation of Efficacy and Safety
rules.verbatim_quote: When biosimilar comparability has been demonstrated in one indication, extrapolation of clinical data to other indications of the reference product could be acceptable, but needs to be scientifically justified... Additional data are required in certain situations, such as: 1. the active substance of the reference product interacts with several receptors that may have a different impact in the tested and non-tested therapeutic indications; 2. the active substance itself has more than one active site and the sites may have a different impact in different therapeutic indications; 3. the studied therapeutic indication is not relevant for the others in terms of efficacy or safety.

rules.rule_text: Clinical safety of biosimilars must be monitored closely on an ongoing basis during the post-approval phase including continued benefit-risk assessment; a pharmacovigilance system description and risk management plan must be presented at authorisation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data from pre-authorisation clinical studies are usually insufficient to identify rare adverse effects. Therefore, clinical safety of biosimilars must be monitored closely on an ongoing basis during the post-approval phase including continued benefit-risk assessment. Within the authorisation procedure the applicant should present a description of the pharmacovigilance system and a risk management plan in accordance with current EU legislation and pharmacovigilance guidelines.

rules.rule_text: All appropriate measures must be taken to identify clearly any biological medicinal product which is the subject of a suspected adverse reaction report, with due regard to its brand name and batch number.
rules.verbatim_quote: For suspected adverse reactions relating to biological medicinal products, the definite identification of the concerned product with regard to its manufacturing is of particular importance. Therefore, all appropriate measures should be taken to identify clearly any biological medicinal product which is the subject of a suspected adverse reaction report, with due regard to its brand name and batch number.
